content: read reviews/691.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/691.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/691.md.

    50→
    51→The problem states a direct assertion: "a necessary and sufficient condition for A to be a Behrend sequence is ∑ 1/a = ∞." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion, no yes/no question, no "find a value").

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/691.lean — Erdős Problem 691

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 691`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/691](https://www.erdosproblems.com/691)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Behrend sequences, multiples, pairwise coprime characterization).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er79e]` with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos691 ... end Erdos691`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos691`.
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open Filter` and `open scoped BigOperators Topology`.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `countMultiples`: has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `IsBehrend`: has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `SetPairwiseCoprime`: has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `ReciprocalSumDiverges`: has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `countMultiples` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `∃ a ∈ A` over an arbitrary `Set ℕ`, which is not decidable).
- [x] `IsBehrend`, `SetPairwiseCoprime`, `ReciprocalSumDiverges` return `Prop` and do not require `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsBehrend`, `SetPairwiseCoprime`, `ReciprocalSumDiverges`.
- [x] Function returning a non-Prop type uses `lowerCamelCase`: `countMultiples`.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. *(Optional — checklist says "consider adding".)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_691` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- Erdős Problem 691 (pairwise coprime case) [Er79e]: ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[Er79e]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem states a direct assertion: "a necessary and sufficient condition for A to be a Behrend sequence is ∑ 1/a = ∞." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion, no yes/no question, no "find a value").

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (`IsBehrend A ↔ ReciprocalSumDiverges A`) — no `answer()` wrapper, as expected for Case 3.
- [x] Parameters `(A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∀ a ∈ A, a ≥ 2) (hCoprime : SetPairwiseCoprime A)` are fine — the quantifier-after-answer rule only applies to `answer()` cases.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `research solved`.
- [x] Value `research solved` is valid (solved but not formally verified).
- [x] Not `research open`, so no conflict with `sorry` in proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` pattern (multi-line: `:= by` on line 70, `sorry` indented on line 71). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present; only the main theorem for the pairwise coprime case.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∣`, `↔`, `≥`, `≠`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
- [ ] Custom linter checks not verified (`linter.style.copyright`, `linter.style.category_attribute`, `linter.style.ams_attribute`, `linter.style.answer_attribute`, `linter.style.namespace`).

---

## Summary

**PASS.** The file is well-structured and conforms to all applicable checklist items. The pairwise coprime case is correctly formalized as a direct assertion (Case 3) with category `research solved`. All four custom definitions have docstrings and appropriate naming conventions. The only unchecked items are optional (`@[category test]` sanity checks for definitions) and build verification (not run). No issues found.

content.text: The review says **PASS** — no changes needed.
